name: Strong-CP problem: theta_QCD bound from neutron EDM
requirement: Any candidate framework must explain or constrain the QCD theta angle, theta_QCD < ~10^-10, derived from the neutron-EDM upper bound through theta_QCD * (m_q / Lambda_QCD). The smallness of theta_QCD relative to its naturally O(1) range is the strong-CP problem; addressing it is one of the canonical motivations for the QCD axion.

regime.description: QCD scale; CP-violating topological term in the QCD Lagrangian L_theta = (theta g_s^2 / 32 pi^2) G_munu G_tilde^munu.

current_bound.units: dimensionless (radians; value is upper bound on |theta|)
current_bound.experiment: Derived from PSI nEDM 2020 upper bound |d_n| < 1.8e-26 e cm
current_bound.reference: Pospelov and Ritz, 'Electric dipole moments as probes of new physics', Annals Phys. 318, 119 (2005), arXiv:hep-ph/0504231; Abel et al. (nEDM at PSI), PRL 124, 081803 (2020), arXiv:2001.11966

what_it_excludes: Naturalness arguments suggest theta_QCD = O(1); the observed bound at 10^-10 demands an explanation. The leading proposal is the Peccei-Quinn mechanism with a dynamical axion. Other proposed solutions (massless up quark, Nelson-Barr mechanism, parity-symmetric BSM) all require new physics. Any candidate framework must specify which.

parameterization.name: Direct: dimensionless QCD theta angle as parameter of the strong-interaction Lagrangian.
parameterization.reference: Peccei and Quinn, PRL 38, 1440 (1977); Weinberg, PRL 40, 223 (1978); Wilczek, PRL 40, 279 (1978).
parameterization.summary: Theta is a free parameter of QCD that can be rotated into an O(1) phase in the quark mass matrix. PQ mechanism: introduce a chiral U(1)_PQ symmetry whose anomaly cancels theta, leaving the axion as Goldstone.

procedural.predicted_observable: theta-induced contribution to the neutron EDM: d_n_theta = e * theta * (m_*) * (chiral coefficient), where m_* = m_u m_d / (m_u + m_d). Lattice and chiral perturbation theory give |d_n / theta| ~ 1.5 x 10^-16 e cm.
framework_inputs: If the framework solves the strong-CP problem dynamically (PQ axion), specify the axion decay constant f_a and the model-dependent E/N anomaly coefficient.
If the framework instead realizes parity-symmetric or Nelson-Barr structures, specify how the resulting theta is rendered small without an axion.

primary_references.citation: Pospelov and Ritz, 'Electric dipole moments as probes of new physics', Annals Phys. 318, 119 (2005)
Peccei and Quinn, 'CP Conservation in the Presence of Pseudoparticles', PRL 38, 1440 (1977)
Abel et al. (nEDM at PSI), 'Measurement of the Permanent Electric Dipole Moment of the Neutron', PRL 124, 081803 (2020)
